mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations, directly compressing forward S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive correlation with FEDFUNDS levels in >65% of monthly observations over a 5-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce corporate earnings expectations, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain positive in >70% of instances where UNRATE crosses the 0.5pp threshold, or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real sales of final goods and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and business demand growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative 20-day returns in >60% of triggered months, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample regression.

mechanism_narrative: Declines in the index of industrial production reflect immediate supply-side bottlenecks that compress short-term corporate output and trigger negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >65% of months where INDPRO declines, or the signal's predictive power vanishes when FEDFUNDS is below 2.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls indicate expanding economic activity and rising wage expenditures that support corporate revenue growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Monthly PAYEMS growth shows negative or zero correlation with 20-day forward returns across all tested macro regimes over a 10-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price index levels above 200 trigger expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which increases equity discount rates and forces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in >60% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds 200, or the threshold effect disappears when FEDFUNDS is already above 5.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between quarterly GDP growth and monthly real sales of final goods signals demand fragmentation that reduces earnings visibility, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The divergence metric yields positive 60-day returns in >65% of observations, or the interaction coefficient is statistically insignificant in out-of-sample forecasting models.

mechanism_narrative: Synchronized growth in industrial production and nonfarm payrolls confirms robust supply-side capacity utilization that supports corporate margins, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative 20-day returns in >60% of triggered instances, or the predictive accuracy drops below baseline when UNRATE exceeds 5.0%.
